feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: >shows up in your Eggers movie he's like the film equivalent of Patches from Dar…
entry.content: >shows up in your Eggers movie he's like the film equivalent of Patches from Dark Souls
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: ITT: State one of a game's biggest flaws or low points, but don't say what game …
entry.content: ITT: State one of a game's biggest flaws or low points, but don't say what game it is. See if others can guess what game it is. For example: In this game, you are subjected to a literal week long tutorial that plays the same single music track over and over.
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Should I get upset or take it as a complement if I get banned from a game for be…

entry.title: /o/ Auto: /ovg/ - long summer break edition
entry.content: Welcome to the Auto Vidya General, the thread for discussion of racing and driving games. No chinkshit peripherals or or ecelebs. Previous thread >>29020673 >Wiki https://get-good.net >Mumble IP: mumble.get-good.net Port: 64738 Password: 4ch >Chat https://chat.get-good.net >Equipment Guide https://docs.google.com/spreadsheet s/d/1uLKMbKwweUZ3QvFeNs8UeycZbDyo0M cxGj7T6uTaTNo >Check the doc for the latest info. GTA Online Rally September 12th https://docs.google.com/spreadsheet s/d/1vlVjNh8r6o_KYU
entry.dc:creator: /o/ Auto

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: SNES
entry.content: Out of every single console in existence, this one is just the best. The controller is amazing. The games are amazing. The graphics are amazing. The sound is amazing. The only issue I have is that it doesn't play NES carts, but it can play gameboy carts with the super gameboy. But still, amazing system. Easily the best.
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /mu/ Music: A step in the right direction, but only a step. It sounds like outtakes from Pla…
entry.content: A step in the right direction, but only a step. It sounds like outtakes from Plastic Ono Band and Imagine, which may not seem so bad but means that Lennon is falling back on ideas that have lost their freshness for him. Still, the single works and I hope he keeps on stepping. Favorite Plastic Ono Band outtake: "One Day (at a Time)." Favorite Imagine outtake: "You Are Here." C+
entry.dc:creator: /mu/ Music

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: TLC New Reality Show About Whores
entry.content: “On the surface, it may look like we are typical housewives, but we’re not,” one housewife says in the trailer. “Behind our perfect suburban lives are the secrets nobody sees.” TLC is the network we all know and love for reality TV that makes fun of retards. THEY'RE BACK TLC is BACK with a new hit show about whores. Let's see some mid life crisis 38 year old who fucks all her husbands friends and uploads it to the Internet, she's getting a show LOL Source https://people.com/tlc-double-lives -sub
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Are you guys ready for the AI singularity takeoff? With AI getting impressive su…
entry.content: Are you guys ready for the AI singularity takeoff? With AI getting impressive superhuman cybercapabilities, surpassing experts in many fields, proving and disproving famous mathematical conjectures and even it starting to have a hand in the process of post training new models, I don't see why it can't happen.
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /mu/ Music: >tfw without this queer Jew and his financial acumen The Beatles would've remain…
entry.content: >tfw without this queer Jew and his financial acumen The Beatles would've remained an obscure cult act that would probably be rediscovered before 2016 on /mu/ and boybandfag would have nothing to write about
entry.dc:creator: /mu/ Music

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Mr. Ono Lennon says the internet has degraded the value of music in people's liv…
entry.content: Mr. Ono Lennon says the internet has degraded the value of music in people's lives, since it's all widely available basically for free. He's right. Yes, it's convenient, but it comes at a cost. It's like how taking pictures has changed. You would only bring a camera to special occasions, for commemorating something or documenting milestones. In the 1800s, people used to need a photographer to do it. Now we all have cameras with us at all times, and it's great and useful, but photos undeniably ho
entry.dc:creator: /mu/ Music

entry.title: /mu/ Music: >churn out Spotify, Max Martin Zara Larsson, and countless shitty EDM djs name a…

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Suicide Would Be Quicker and Less Painful
entry.content: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=quS lN6g-v1E >ChatGPT broke containment from a sandbox and committed a felony using previously undiscovered software vulnerabilities in order to steal test answers Will there be a global moratorium on AI development? You bet your ass not! You getting killed by my AI is A-OK when muh company's stock evaluations are in jeopardy. You can just sit and: enjoy having your jobs taken, enjoy having your content and customer service replaced with slop, enjoy being incorrec
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Cultural Technology
entry.content: ITT we post pieces of technology that aren't universal, items that are unique to various cultures. The chukudu is a remarkable, hand-built wooden vehicle that serves as a vital economic lifeline in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo, particularly around the city of Goma. Born out of necessity due to poor road infrastructure and the high cost of motorized transport, these robust, unmotorized scooters are ingeniously crafted from local wood and scrap metal, designed to carry massive load
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /g/ Technology: AI facial recognition
entry.content: Cops in my country are using ClearView AI to catch criminals. The AI has scraped social media for millions of pics of people's faces. How is that legal? Sure, I have an online presence. I put my photos online willingly. But I have never given consent for governments to steal my photos and use them for training material that is used in the legal system of all places. Actually, how is any AI scraping legal? Governments and companies have scraped 100% of the Internet and are using it to make Minori
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Post bands/duos that never reunited. I'll start: The Beatles: 1960–1970 The Smit…
entry.content: Post bands/duos that never reunited. I'll start: The Beatles: 1960–1970 The Smiths: 1982–1987 The Jam: 1972–1982 Thompson Twins: 1977–1993 Japan: 1974–1982 (not sure if the regrouping under another name counts) REM: 1980–2011 (not sure if their two get togethers should count) Siouxsie & the Banshees: 1976–1996 (their Wiki entry was like this for years, now it says 2002 reunion, does it count) Hall & Oates: 1970–2022 The Kinks: 1962–1997 Cocteau Twins: 1979–1997 XTC: 1972–2006 The White Stripes:
entry.dc:creator: /mu/ Music

entry.title: /v/ Video Games: Popular Character Polls
entry.content: Japanese magazines often releases polls about the most popular video game characters in Japan, but I never saw something similar in the West. Is there any old video game magazine or gaming website which did such a poll outside Japan? If you don't know of any, answer this question then: do you think an hypotetical Western-centric poll would be similar to Japan's?
entry.dc:creator: /v/ Video Games

entry.title: /g/ Technology: >BPFDoor is a Linux based passive long-term backdoor used by China-based threat …
entry.content: >BPFDoor is a Linux based passive long-term backdoor used by China-based threat actors. First seen in 2021, BPFDoor is named after its usage of Berkley Packet Filter (BPF) to execute single task instructions. BPFDoor supports multiple protocols for communicating with a C2 including TCP, UDP, and ICMP and can start local or reverse shells that bypass firewalls using iptables. https://attack.mitre.org/software/S 1161/ CommonSense™ me arse...
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Songs with Epic Intros
entry.content: I want to know if there are other songs like the following: "Foreplay/Longtime" by Boston "Funeral for a Friend/Love Lies" by Elton John "I Need a Lover" by John Mellencamp I distinctly like the following things about these: -feel like two separate pieces of music put together, but it works -the intro isnt single instruments and has the full band -no lyrics in the first half -very dynamic, almost prog writing in the intro half -catchy almost pop sensibilities in the 2nd half -prefer single track
entry.dc:creator: /mu/ Music

entry.title: /mu/ Music: Not to get too esoteric on you, but Barnett reminds me of Perry Como, who croone…
entry.content: Not to get too esoteric on you, but Barnett reminds me of Perry Como, who crooned exactly 100 hits in pre-rock 'n' roll 1943-1954 and kept going till 1960 and beyond, including a number-two 1955 cover of Gene and Eunice's "Ko Ko Mo" and the number-one 1956 teen-targeted "Hot Diggity (Dog Ziggity Boom)." Como hosted his long-running TV show in a cardigan, was married to the same woman for 65 years, and died of Alzheimer's complications in 2001, and though as a kid I thought he was boring, I now a
entry.dc:creator: /mu/ Music

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Causal Permeability Principle
entry.content: Missing distinction in discussions about self-improving AI: A system can: - rewrite its own code - optimize its weights - generate new tools - improve its performance and still be trapped inside the rules that determine how it changes. Proposed criterion: P_C ≡ E* C_rev where: E* = external reality / incoming causal information C_rev = mechanism that determines how the system modifies its own future modification process = causal reachability Claim: A genuinely open-ended AI system requires that
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /mu/ Music: >The most classic of British synth-pop bands, and also one of the longest-lived …
entry.content: >The most classic of British synth-pop bands, and also one of the longest-lived (but perhaps also the most overrated), Depeche Mode—featuring Dave Gahan (vocals), Andy Fletcher (keyboards), Vince Clarke (keyboards), and Martin Gore (keyboards)—emerged during the romantic and futuristic trends of the early 1980s. The style they invented is one of the least original of that era, but also one of the easiest to digest, often echoing Tamla soul and Merseybeat—the most conservative genres of the 1960s
entry.dc:creator: /mu/ Music

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Twongi 2001, meme or a shitposter's best friend?
entry.content: Europoor here. Is a used twongi (picrel) worth it as a first car? I'm looking out for something compact that wouldn't be a money pit (so a smart fortwo wouldnt fit the bill) I am also long overdue to learn my way around an engine, I would love to learn how to perform basic maintenance and repairs. The fewer sensors I have to deal with, the better. talk me out of it, /o/
entry.dc:creator: /o/ Auto

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Is software "engineering" a legitimate field? Be honest. Me mum vibe coded a mar…
entry.content: Is software "engineering" a legitimate field? Be honest. Me mum vibe coded a marketplace app in a weekend and is making money from it. How can that be a serious industry in 2026?

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Why weren't V8 Diesels popular in passenger cars?
entry.content: AFAIK the only large scale production of V8 Diesels in passenger cars were the 3.6 and 4.4 TDV8/SDV8 engines used in the L322 and L405 Range Rover and Range Rover Sport >inb4 BMW, Mercedes and Audi had V8 diesels These sold nowhere near on the scale that the Land Rover V8 Diesel sold, and were niche, even at the time
entry.dc:creator: /o/ Auto

entry.title: /g/ Technology: The CMP 170HX seems like a scam
entry.content: My understanding is these were always effective versions of higher cards that didn't meet quality. A practice nvidia has done forever. They then lock these defective chips in the bios. We've known this forever, but its been hard to unlock. Now some absolute Chad thundercock has cracked it, and we are seeing 40gb and unstable 64gbs on $200 cards. Just like the tesla p40 situation. But these chips are by their nature effective. They will not be stable otherwise nvidia would have sold them at the n
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /o/ Auto: De Tomaso P72
entry.content: IT'S HAPPENING? After 7 years, the first P72 is finally rolling around Yuro roads and it's beautiful >Its “Aurelian Night” paint—a deep blue designed to evoke the transition from day to night—completely changes its appearance depending on the light. The numerous hand-polished rose gold accents—from the wheels and side mirrors to the trim and body details—further enhance its unique character
entry.dc:creator: /o/ Auto

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Yet another devvie yt channel
entry.content: Who is interested in yet another development channel? Think tsoding or whatever. I wanna focus on AI shit, real dystopia beat. We're all fucked anyway, might as well have some of the fun cyberpunk toys. No retarded gimmicks, just code, demo, discussion, the usual shit.

entry.content: What's the general consensus on these? Fuel additives are generally regarded as snake oil are these as well or do they actually do something?

entry.title: /o/ Auto: Financed POS as a brokie
entry.content: Anyone else thought financing a car meant smooth sailing for half a decade only for the fucking thing to constantly need work that you can't really afford on top of the monthly payments and full coverage insurance? Just me? It's a 2021 toyota corolla that disintegrates from commuting to work. I'm paying at minimum $900 a month and it's not even driveable right now. I have to take a shitbox with 300,000 miles to work instead. This is so goddamn retarded.
entry.dc:creator: /o/ Auto

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Important news
entry.content: Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey continues to dominate online discussions, but not all of the attention has been positive. The film's official trailer has now amassed 910,300 reported dislikes on YouTube, bringing it close to the one-million mark and highlighting the intense debate surrounding one of the most anticipated films in recent years. Despite the controversy, the trailer has attracted an impressive 11.59 million views, along with 106,021 likes and more than 46,700 comments, demonstrating
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: >Black Hank is a stepdad of a white son which is why he doesn't understand him a…
entry.content: >Black Hank is a stepdad of a white son which is why he doesn't understand him and his Fortnite dancin' ways. "DAD WATCH THIS" >Dale is a black israelite who says the white man was invented by Yakub, and Hank has to protect his boy Bobby from his crazy beliefs >Black Bill runs a barbershop after his dad got shot when he fucked up someones fade (a result of his arthritis). Instead of depression over his wife, he deals with having to live in his father's shadow >Black Boomhauer is a downlow homo t
entry.dc:creator: /tv/ TV & Film
